feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Build
feed.subtitle: SAP Build Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How use HTTP destination request with GOS?
entry.content:
Hello,
I have an issue that I am facing with a button functionality implemented using the Flow
Function ‘HTTP destination request’ in SAP Build Apps.


Context
I am developing an application in SAP Build Apps that needs to upload attachments to
S/4HANA using the standard OData service API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV.
The upload is triggered by a button press in the UI, and the attachment is selected by the
end user.
I am following an SAP blog that explains how to upload attachments from SAP Build Apps
using the HTTP Destination Request component. In the blog, the implementation is done
using DMS (Document Management Service) and the approach works correctly, including
the CSRF handling and multipart/form-data POST request. In my case, I cannot use DMS
and I need to upload attachments using GOS (Generic Object Services) via the standard
OData service API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV. I am trying to adapt the same approach shown
in the blog to GOS, but this is where I am currently blocked with the 403 Forbidden error.


Source: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/connect-sap-build-apps-with-dm
s-free-projects-videos/ba-p/13986096

I used the same destination configuration as described in the blog.

The only difference in the destination details is the Proxy Type, which is set to ‘Internet’ and in my case is OnPremise.

------

In the Request body i send key: media with value: <picture jpg>

Path: /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV/AttachmentContentSet
Headers with the relative value:
● Slug: (Slug of the image)
● LinkedSAPObjectKey: (LinkedSAPObjectKey data from a Notification created
before this)
● BusinessObjectTypeName: BUS2038
● Content-Type: image/jpeg

If I press F12 and look at the network tab I can see the Response, the error 403 Forbidden.

But then If I tried with Postman it works.

And on the S/4HANA usign the transaction /IWFND/TRACES I can see an error exactly on
the moment when a HEAD request is executed


Someone have a clue about this?
Thanks

entry.title: Is SAP Build subscription alone sufficient to create mobile applications, or is SAP Build Apps subsc
entry.content:
As per SAP documentation, SAP Build is a bundle that includes the following services:

  1. SAP Build Apps

  2. SAP Build Process Automation

  3. SAP Build Work Zone

I would like to clarify the licensing and subscription aspect.

If we subscribe only to SAP Build, is that sufficient to create and deploy mobile applications using SAP Build Apps?

Or do we need to purchase/subscribe to SAP Build Apps separately, even though it is part of the SAP Build bundle?

It would be helpful if someone could explain:

Whether SAP Build Apps is automatically included with SAP Build

Or if it requires an individual subscription/entitlement

Any limitations (users, runtime, mobile deployment) with SAP Build Apps under SAP Build

Thanks in advance for your guidance.

entry.title: BPA Unable to verify the signature of the archive (Transport Joule Skills)
entry.content:
Hi guys, could anyone help me out?

In SAP Build Process Automation (BPA), I’m trying to migrate/transport a Joule Skill project from Subaccount A (US, Azure) to Target Subaccount B (another region, US VA, AWS).

The import fails with this error: 

Unable to verify the signature of the archive. Make sure the landscape keys from landscape us21-prod are imported in your landscape, if not, contact DevOps. Ensure that the other potential reasons for this error are checked. This error could happen for the following reasons: Couldn't get key xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx version from credential store.

The same error - Transport and Upload proyect.

 

I’ve searched everywhere and couldn’t find anything useful.

I did find an SAP Note that suggests using a tenant in the same region, which is honestly very limiting because it would force us to change our entire landscape design just because of BPA:

 

 
https://me.sap.com/notes/3472593/E

Has anyone found a way to solve this (e.g., importing landscape keys / fixing credential store key access) and successfully transport BPA/Joule projects across regions?

I already opened a ticket with SAP, but I haven’t received an answer yet.

Thanks a lot!

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I'm working on SAP Build Process Automation, here I'm working on document information extraction and making use of a document template, I have created data types and also extracted invoice data. 

The next step is where I'm creating invoice variable, here when I'm clicking create custom data option, it is not working.

Attaching the screenshot for your reference. Kindly do the needful as soon as possible.

Thank you so much

Kind Regards

Tazeem R

entry.title: Can a SAP Build Action be setup to stream output to a SAP Joule Skill?
entry.content:
We have an API which streams the AI Reasoning output in real-time.

We can surface the streamed output at a command line by calling our API as follows:

 try:
        with requests.post(
            API_URL,
            json=payload,
            headers=headers,
            stream=True,  # 👈 enables streaming
        ) as response:
        
Ideally, we'd like to present this streamed output in a SAP Joule Skill, with an action for our API. However,  the output is not streamed,  it returns only the final step of the output in one go, so not only is the full response not shown, it also takes longer to appear, which is not a good user experience.

Can Joule Skills/SAP Build Actions handle streamed output and how that acheived?

entry.title: Business Partner creation $batch fails at “Call Web Service” in SAP Build Process Automation (BPA)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am using SAP Build Process Automation (BPA) together with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition to execute SAP’s standard template “Mass Create of Business Partners (58Z)”.
My goal is to create Business Partners via the API_BUSINESS_PARTNER OData service using a $batch POST request.

However, the automation fails at the Call Web Service step with the following error:

irpa_core.error.RequestError: Response code 401 (Unauthorized)

The earlier steps run successfully; the issue occurs only when BPA executes the actual $batch POST for Business Partner creation.

Environment

  • Target system: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Main automation tool: SAP Build Process Automation (BPA)
  • Scenario: Create Business Partners via API_BUSINESS_PARTNER $batch
  • Template:  Mass Maintenance of Business Partners (58Z)

 


Current problem

  • BPA reaches the Call Web Service activity that performs:
    POST https://<tenant>-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/$batch
  • At this moment, BPA throws:
    401 Unauthorized (irpa_core.error.RequestError)

    Additional data I can provide (sanitized)

    • Communication User / System / Arrangement screenshots
    • BPA logs for HEAD and Call Web Service (request/response headers)
    • If needed, I can test in Postman using the same Communication User and share:
      • GET A_BusinessPartner?$top=1
      • HEAD x-csrf-token: fetch
      • POST $batch

    This may be a basic question, but any guidance or clarification would be greatly appreciated.


This may be a basic question, but any guidance or clarification would be greatly appreciated. 

—Moon14

entry.title: How to get the approver when a form is send for approval to multiple recipients in SAP BPA Workflow
entry.content: In sap BPA workflow, I am sending for for approval form to multiple recipients. I want to get the the recipient who took the action made the form Approve/Rejected. Whoever took the action. So as to update the recipient details to System.

entry.title: SmartForm Recovery – Request for Assistance
entry.content:
We recently made some changes to the SmartForm, and those changes have been transported to the PRD system. However, the client is now requesting the previous format.

Could you please let us know if it is possible to recover or restore the earlier version of the SmartForm?

I also attempted to re-import the old transport request from DEV to QA, but the previous changes were not reflected.
May I kindly request you to prioritize this and provide a solution as soon as possible.
Regards,
Jayachitra.

entry.title: Cannot deploy agent in Joule Studio (Agent creation failed)
entry.content:
I am experiencing this issue when trying to deploy a custom Joule agent to a shared environment:

Deployment of Joule skills are working fine. We have AI Units available so I am not sure what causes the issue, are there some additional configuration steps necessary to allow Joule agents the consumption of AI Units before deployment (my guess is the problem is related to AI units since the deployment of skills seems to work fine).

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation, My Processes App not working as intended
entry.content:
I am not sure if the My Processes App from Build Process Automation is functioning correctly in Shared Environments.

I was under the assumption that with this app it is possible for users with the ProcessAutomationParticipant role to look at the status of processes in which they have been involved in, as for example, approvers.

I am not able to view any processes in which I am involved in from shared environments that are not owned by me, unless I give myself the Supervise Authorization which leads to me being able to view all processes from that environment, not just the ones I was involved in.

Is this intended? What is the setup so users are only able to only see the processes in which they have been involved, that are running on a shared environment?

entry.title: BTP BuildApps- Error handling and logging possibility
entry.content:
Hello community,

Currently,  we are developing Build Apps as mashup/extension for Sales and Service Cloud V2 or C4C V2.

Now we have use case to handle the address validation logic in Build Apps where the external APIs are consumed, that clearly means the APIs could give us different errors/exceptions in case any issue occurred from external system. How can we log and monitor errors occurred in Build Apps from external systems ?

Examples:
Network & Connectivity Errors: Timeouts, DNS failures, and connection resets.
Authentication/Authorization Errors: 401 Unauthorized, 403 Forbidden, invalid or expired API keys

Rate Limits and Quotas: Exceeding allowed request volume
Data Format/Schema Mismatches: API changes its data structure
API Provider Downtime (5xx Errors): 500 Internal Server Error (server crash) or 503 Service Unavailable (server overload)
Invalid URL/Endpoint Changes: The provider updates their documentation or removes an endpoint without notice

Can you guide us how to handle errors in Build Apps so that we can take advantage of logging and monitoring?
Thank you.
Willem_Pardaens @vvdries @gregorw @qmacro @martinstenzig

entry.title: Automation around Transports (STMS_IMPORT)
entry.content:
Hello guys,

I am wondering whether somebody from community has "good" experience with automation transport requests in SAP. I am talking about technical transports requests (STMS), not logistics.

In the past I build a process automation with a form where selected users could enter a transport request. The bot checked that request for some preconditions and imported it then into a SAP system.

In the meantime and with much more experience I doubt whether this scenario is really good for a "bot". The check of the preconditions is really complex, because the request is only allowed to have special objects included and nothing else. I am importing with BAPI. There I only can start the import but don't get a reliable feedback (especially for huge requests). Also there are no BAPI for STMS_QA, so I don't get this list clean.

I also noticed that SAP itself doesn't have any automation around STMS_IMPORT in her portfolio (store). That must also have some reasons because there are over 600 projects included and STMS uses nearly every customer.

In the past we dealt that topic with tickets and a manual import from "IT guys".

What are your thoughts about that issue?

entry.title: Insufficient Privilege while creating agent environment in SAP Build Control Tower
entry.content:
Hello, 

As part of working on this tutorial on step 5, I want to create a new environment. I have assigned all the process automation roles - process automation admin, developer, expert etc. But still not able to create the environment and getting the error below.

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-setup-desktop-3-0-agent.html

Can you please help.

Thanks,

Mainak

entry.title: SAP HANA cloud instance dropdown empty
entry.content:
I've added a SAP HANA Cloud instance (tried both hana-free and hana-td) to my BTP subaccount. I can access these using SAP HANA Cloud Central. Instances are using the Cloud Foundry Environment of my subaccount.

In SAP Build, created a FullStack dev-space:

Create my project using Full-Stack Node.js Application.

In SAP Business Application Studio, create a local CDS view with some data. All is working fine.

Now want to use the HANA Cloud instance. For this, I click in the left menu on "Run Configuration". For the database, toggle from "Local (projecs default)" to "SAP HANA Cloud" and login to Cloud Foundry using SSO.
Once done, I can select my Cloud Foundry Organinization (which is my subaccount) and I can select the Cloud Foundry Space "dev". Then, when I need to select the SAP HANA Cloud Instance, the dropdown stays empty:

First thought that I needed to add a distination in BTP. So, on the HANA instance created a service key and went to Connectivity -> Destinations.

However, not sure what to fill in for the URL as in the service key, the URL starts with:

So entered the URL found earlier in the JSON wich starts with "https://sapbuild-xxxxxxxx.authentication.....".

I must be overlooking or missing something. Does someone please help me out?

 

Edit 3/16/2026: One thing I've noticed is this popping up:

Don't know on how to fix this.

entry.title: How to remove/delete form from SAP Build Process Automation Inbox after due date?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am working with SAP Build Process Automation and have a requirement related to task/forms in the Inbox.

Once a task reaches its due date, I want to automatically remove or delete the associated form/task from the SAP Build Inbox.

Currently, even after the due date is passed, the form still remains visible in the Inbox.

Could you please help with the following:

  • Is there a way to automatically delete or archive forms after the due date?

  • Can this be handled using workflow configuration, timers, or any automation?

  • Are there any best practices to manage expired tasks in the Inbox?

I have already explored workflow settings but could not find an option for automatic removal.

Any guidance or recommended approach would be helpful.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: how SAP Build user license consumtion works
entry.content:
Dear community,

Last one month I am trying to identify how SAP build user license consumption works.

Here is my latest subscriptions. Earlier i subscribed SAP Build Process automation with Build-default as it is a prerequisites for joule skills availability for building skills in joule studio.

The reason why removed SAP BPA build-default subscription is that SAP started charging abnormal SAP build user ( developer ) license cost belong to SKU - 8019311 as shown below for which i never subscribed. After continuous follow up for almost a month i could recover the credits back but it impacted our development since SAP didn't explained entirely how they calculated the consumption and without their proper explanation we don't want to end up in the same situation so dropped the subscription and went ahead with free subscription there by sacrificing joule skills testing.  

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-service/build-service-guide/service-plans-and-metering

 

As i mentioned above when SAP BPA subscription is build-default and SAP build code - free , SAP BAS - free then how the SAP build user (developer ) license will work out. If SAP calculates the SAP Build charges based on SAP BPA build-default plan then there is no proper documentation where it should mention that go with only SAP BPA as it brings all the functionality of SAP build with it start building you apps and skills and we will start charging for it and no need of other subscriptions.

Please go through it and provide me your observations.

Thanks and Regards

entry.title: How to: Use OpenText Core Archive for Document Storage/Archiving from SAP BTP / Build?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We are designing a solution on SAP BTP using SAP Build / CAP and SAP Build Process Automation and would like to use OpenText Core Archive for SAP Solutions as our document storage/archiving platform – without involving an SAP ECC/S/4HANA backend for the document upload.

Scenario:

  • We have a SAP Fiori / CAP front-end application with a file upload (PDF) functionality deployed in Cloud Foundry / BTP.
  • Uploaded PDFs should:
    1. Be stored/archived centrally in OpenText Core Archive for SAP Solutions.
    2. Be retrievable later in SAP Build Process Automation to show the document (PDF) in an approval form for review/approval.
  • We want to call OpenText directly from BTP, not via ECC/S/4HANA, and then access those documents via APIs from workflows in SAP Build Process Automation.

Questions:

  • OpenText Core Archive APIs from BTP

    • Does OpenText Core Archive for SAP Solutions expose REST APIs that can be called directly from BTP (CAP/Node/Java) for:
      • Creating (uploading) documents (PDF) with metadata?
      • Retrieving documents (either as binary or via a time-limited URL)?
  • Or is there a more “standard” pattern for using Core Archive as a central document store from BTP without ECC/S/4?
  • Are there any official guidelines or recommendations from SAP/OpenText for this type of BTP ↔ Core Archive integration?
  • Even if examples are for Extended ECM or other OpenText products, any patterns applicable to Core Archive would be helpful.

Any inputs, experiences, example architectures, or links to documentation/blogs are highly appreciated.

Thanks! in Advance.

BR,
Rakesh

entry.title: SBPA Destination Error: Destination not found.
entry.content:
I am following below tutorial from developer.sap.com and getting destination connectivity issue.
https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-apps-workflow-trigger.html

{error: "Error: Destination not found.↵", message: "Destination not found."}
error: "Error: Destination not found.\n" 
message: "Destination not found."

Steps

1. I have completed Create a Sales Order Process Using SAP Build Process Automation Successfully.

2. After that I have created Create a Sales Order App Using SAP Build Apps from SAP Build Successfully.

3. When I try to access from Build App Preview getting above destination issue.

4. As per tutorial it will work like this.

Regards,

Abderrahmane Kermiche

entry.title: Trigger SAP Build Process Automation Workflow from SAP Build Apps (Form & API Based)
entry.content:
Trigger SAP Build Process Automation Workflow from SAP Build Apps (Form & API Based)

When building applications using SAP Build Apps, a common requirement is to trigger workflows created in SAP Build Process Automation (BPA).

However, many developers face confusion:

Can we trigger a Form-based process directly from Build Apps?

👉 Short answer: No — Build Apps can only trigger workflows via API.
But don’t worry — there’s a proper workaround.

In this blog, we’ll cover:

  • ✅ Triggering API-based workflows
  • ✅ Triggering Form-based workflows via API workaround
  • ✅ End-to-end configuration

Understanding the Problem

In SAP Build Process Automation:

Trigger TypeCan Build Apps Trigger Directly?
Form TriggerNo
API TriggerYes

Reason:
Build Apps communicates via REST APIs, not UI forms.

ℹ️To Integrate Build Apps with Process Automation we need BTP Destination
Create Destination in SAP BTP with the same Name "sap_process_automation_service_user_access". Authentication can be either OAuth2ClientCredentials or OAuth2JWTBearer but add the correct Client ID and Client Secret ID from Service key from the instance of SAP Build Process Automation. Additional Properties are important to get the success integration. Finally save and check the connection.

Approach 1: API-Based Workflow Trigger 

Step 1: Create Process with API Trigger

  • Open your BPA project
  • Create a Process
  • Select Start Event → API Trigger

    Define Input Schema:
    {
    "FirstName": "string"
    }

Step 2: Deploy the Process

Click the three dots in SAP lobby of deployed process and publish to library. So that only you can integrate deployed process in SAP Build Apps.

After deployment, you will get:

  • Workflow API endpoint
  • Process Definition ID

Step 3: Configure SAP Build Apps

In SAP Build Apps:

1.Go to App Settings

Click Enable Authentication, Choose SAP BTP Authentication. Click Ok on the Popup.
If already SAP BTP Authentication added in App Setting Leave the Above Step

2.Integrate Workflow Process

Go to Integrations Tab Click Add Integration Button, Choose SAP Build Library. Under Processes you can select the published workflow process and  Enable process

3.Create UI

  1. Input fields:
    -> FirstName
  2. Start Process Button
    Add Logic to the above Button.

In Logic window, Add Trigger Process, choose the enabled Process and configure the input parameter as object with properties. To show status you can add either Alert/ Toast Message.
     

✅ Result

  • User give inputs in Build Apps
  • Clicks Submit
  • Workflow starts in BPA

Approach 2: Form-Based Process 

⚠️ Important Concept

Even if your process uses a Form Start Event,
👉 there is NO direct API to submit that form

So this will NOT work:
❌ Build Apps → Submit BPA Form directly

✅ Correct Workaround

🔄Convert Form Input → API Payload in SAP Build Apps

Step 1: Create Form Based Trigger Workflow in SAP Build Process Automation.

    • Here I took Leave Approval Application Workflow and deploy it. Trigger the process once then you can see the definition Id in Monitoring tab for this triggered workflow. Copy that ID for later use.
        

Step 2 : Create Build Apps Application 

  • Add SAP BTP Authentication in App Settings if not you not done yet.
  • Go to Integration tab and create a data entity by selecting SAP BTP Destination Rest API integration.
    • In Base, Choose the destination and add resource schema. ⚠️ Do not add common request header for token passing

    • Click on create and Enable it.
      • Set the relative path and query - Add static value "/public/workflow/rest/v1/workflow-instances".
      • Set Request Method as POST. 
      • Add Request Headers as List -> Key : Content-Type, Value :  application/json.
      • Add Request Body Mapper by wrap the data as json. and Save the data entity.
        ENCODE_JSON({"definitionId":"<value of the definitionId which is copied from Monitoring tab in SAP BPA>", "context": {<all parameters present in startEvent of input schema (you can refer in monitoring tab of the triggered process>}})


  • Create UI with required input fields. Add Button and configure the process flow in logic canvas.
    • Add create record and configure the resource which is create by following above steps. Set the records as object with properties. Add Toast Message/ Alert for your purpose.
    • Test the App, you will get the result and also you can cross check with monitoring tab in SAP BPA for the triggered process

⚠️ Common Issues

❌ Invalid Token 

  • Wrong OAuth configuration
  • No proper Addition Properties

❌ 401 Unauthorized

  • Client ID / Secret issue

❌ Workflow Not Starting

  • Wrong definitionID
  • Incorrect payload format

These can resolve by adding correct input configuration and Correct destination configuration.

🔄 Architecture Overview

SAP Build Apps (UI Form) ➡️ REST API Call (OAuth) ➡️ SAP Build Process Automation ➡️ Workflow Execution
 
🎯 Best Practices
✔ Validate payload before sending
✔ Use destinations for secure connectivity
✔ Always use inspect to check the console for error

Conclusion

🎉 And that’s a wrap!
You’ve now unlocked the power of triggering workflows in SAP Build Process Automation directly from SAP Build Apps using API-driven integration.
What started as a simple requirement—triggering a workflow—has evolved into a powerful architectural pattern:

  • 🎯 Build Apps handling the user experience
  • ⚙️ SAP BPA orchestrating the process logic
  • 🔗 APIs acting as the bridge between them

By moving beyond Form Triggers and embracing an API-first approach, you’re not just solving a limitation—you’re designing a solution that is scalable, flexible, and enterprise-ready.

💡 Imagine the possibilities:
From employee requests and approvals to complex cross-system automations—this pattern can power countless real-world scenarios with efficiency and control.

🚀 And this is just the beginning.
You can take it further by integrating additional services, enhancing validations, or building intelligent, event-driven workflows across your SAP landscape.

If you found this helpful or have ideas to expand this approach, feel free to connect and share your thoughts!

Let’s build smarter automation together.

LinkedIn : https://www.linkedin.com/in/abirami-t/

entry.title: Build Work Zone: No Content Items Created from S/4HANA FLP Content Exposure (0/0)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently integrating SAP Build Work Zone (Standard Edition) with an SAP S/4HANA on‑premise system using Launchpad Content Exposure to SAP BTP (Exposure Version 2).

Although the content exposure in S/4HANA seems to be set up correctly, no content items are created in Build Work Zone after channel synchronization.

Observed Behavior

  • The FLP Content Exposure runs successfully in S/4HANA
  • JSON content is generated for:
    • Business Apps
    • Catalogs
    • Groups
    • Roles
  • In Build Work Zone → Channel Manager, the channel status shows Updated
  • However, the Channel Report shows:

    Content Items: 0 / 0
    No content items were created or updated

Expected Behavior

  • Catalogs, apps, and groups exposed from S/4HANA should be converted into content items in Build Work Zone and become available for site usage.

Configuration Details

  • S/4HANA FLP content includes:
    • Business apps (homepage, search)
    • Catalogs assigned to roles
    • Role created specifically for Build Work Zone consumption
  • Content exposure uses Exposure Version 2
  • Channel is created in Build Work Zone in automatic mode
  • Cloud connector and destination setup is in place

Checks Already Done

  • Verified that FLP catalogs are assigned to the role
  • Verified that the role is included in content exposure
  • Channel synchronization completes without errors
  • No errors in BWZ logs, only empty content result

Questions

  1. Which technical roles are mandatory for the S/4HANA user (or technical integration user) to allow Build Work Zone to read FLP content?
  2. Is any additional role required for content item generation?
  3. Are there additional authorization objects (e.g. /UI2/FLP_CONT) that are required?
  4. Are there known prerequisites or limitations when using Exposure Version 2 with Build Work Zone?
  5. Any checks or logs you would recommend to identify why content items are not being generated?

Any guidance or pointers would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Please find the screenshots below for your reference.

Kind regards,
Om Prakash Jha
